title: Chlamydia, gonorrhea and infectious syphilis in Canada, 2018 - Canada.ca
content:
## Chlamydia, gonorrhea and infectious syphilis in Canada, 2018: Infographic
Download the alternative format (PDF format, 87 KB, 1 pages)
Organization: Public Health Agency of Canada
Date published: 2021-01-29
The Public  Health Agency of Canada collects information on reported cases of the sexually  transmitted infections chlamydia, gonorrhea and infectious syphilis through the  Canadian Notifiable Diseases Surveillance System (CNDSS).
Trends in chlamydia  by age and sex, 2009 to 2018
- There were 117,008 cases of chlamydia  in 2018.
- Between 2009 and 2018, there was a 40%  increase in the rate of reported cases of chlamydia.
Proportion    of cases (%)
- 59.6% of chlamydia cases are reported  among women.
- Rates have increased by 67.6% among  men since 2009.
Rates of reported chlamydia cases per  100,000 population
- The Canadian rate of chlamydia for  2018 was 363.2 per 100,000 people.
- In 2018, rates of chlamydia below the  national rate were reported in Nova Scotia (340.0), Quebec (337.9), Ontario  (332.5), New Brunswick (282.9), Prince Edward Island (267.6) and Newfoundland  and Labrador (183.5).
- In 2018, rates of chlamydia above the  national rate were reported in Alberta (402.0), Manitoba (546.3), Saskatchewan  (552.7), Yukon (699.8), Northwest Territories (1,747.1) and Nunavut (3,965.9).
Trends in gonorrhea  by age and sex, 2009 to 2018
- There were 30,874 cases of gonorrhea  in 2018.
- Between 2009 and 2018, there was a  190% increase in the rate of reported cases of gonorrhea.
Proportion    of cases (%)
- 64.6% of gonorrhea cases are reported  among men.
- Rates have increased by 241.9% among  men since 2009.
Rates of reported gonorrhea cases per  100,000 population (2018)
- The Canadian rate of gonorrhea for  2017 was 95.8 per 100,000 population.
- In 2018, rates of gonorrhea below the  national rate were reported in Quebec (89.7), Ontario (72.4), Yukon (71.5), Nova  Scotia (32.0), Newfoundland and Labrador (13.9), New Brunswick (12.3) and Prince  Edward Island (8.4).
- In 2018, rates of gonorrhea above the  national rate were reported in Alberta (115.9), Saskatchewan (220.8), Manitoba  (265.0), Northwest Territories (1,027.3) and Nunavut (1,911.1).
Trends in infectious  syphilis by age and sex, 2009 to 2018
- There were 6,281 cases of infectious  syphilis in 2018.
- Between 2009 and 2018, there was a 259%  increase in the rate of reported cases of infectious syphilis.
Proportion    of cases (%)
- 78.9% of infectious syphilis cases  are reported among men.
- Rates have increased by 648.3% among  women since 2009
Rates of reported infectious syphilis  cases per 100,000 population (2018)
- The Canadian rate of infectious  syphilis for 2018 was 16.9 per 100,000 population.
- In 2018, rates of infectious syphilis  at or below the national rate were reported in British Columbia (15.2), Ontario  (13.2), Saskatchewan (12.1), Quebec (11.1), Newfoundland and Labrador (7.2),  Yukon (4.9), Nova Scotia (3.3), New Brunswick (2.7) and Prince Edward Island  (2.6).
- In 2018, rates of infectious syphilis  above the national rate were reported in Northwest Territories (24.5), Alberta  (35.7), Manitoba (57.9) and Nunavut (263.7).
Note:  Chlamydia and gonorrhea data for British Columbia were not available for  the year 2018.
For  more information, please visit Notifiable Diseases Online and the 2018 Report on sexually transmitted infections in Canada .
